---
title: "Validation evidence summary"
author: "David Sarrat Gonzalez"
output:
  rmarkdown::html_vignette:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 2
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Validation evidence summary}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

## Purpose

This article is an index and audit summary for the executable validation
vignettes. It does not run a DSLite analysis itself. The method-specific
articles linked below are the executable evidence: each one generates its
dataset in the vignette, partitions it vertically, starts an in-memory DSLite
deployment, aligns records, runs the dsVert route, runs the centralized R
reference on the same pooled data, computes the numerical delta, and asserts
the result.

The current evidence snapshot covers 17 method blocks, both `K=2` and `K>=3`
modalities, and 34 route-level validation rows. All rendered rows are `PASS`.
The route-level suite runs with the negotiated `auto` Beaver preprocessing
policy, which uses the efficient dealer profile when all servers allow it and
automatically switches to IKNP OT-extension when any server requires that
stronger profile. Four additional profile-validation articles run selected
routes twice, once under `dealer` and once under `iknp`, to show that the
preprocessing backend affects runtime rather than the statistical target.

## Validation contract

Each method vignette builds a `rows` data frame with one row per validated
product route. The common columns are:

| Column | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `k_mode` | Whether the route is validated with two peers (`K=2`) or at least three peers (`K>=3`). |
| `function_route` | User-facing `ds.vert.*` route or route option being exercised. |
| `dataset` | The generated fixture used for the comparison. |
| `reference_target` | The centralized R reference used as the comparator. |
| `primary_metric` | The numerical error criterion, usually a max absolute coefficient, probability, loading, or algebra delta. |
| `observed` | The observed distributed-vs-centralized delta. |
| `tolerance` | The accepted validation envelope for the method and route. |
| `tier` | Accuracy/disclosure class used in the validation matrix. |
| `non_disclosive` | Route-level disclosure assertion. Accepted evidence rows must remain `TRUE`. |
| `status` | `PASS` when `observed` is finite and within `tolerance`; otherwise `FAIL`. |

`assert_validation(rows)` is the executable assertion layer at the end of each
method vignette. It does not compute, tune, or change any result. It stops the
render if any row has `status != "PASS"` or if any row is marked
`non_disclosive == FALSE`. Therefore, a rendered method article is evidence
that the route stayed within the declared numerical envelope and passed the
route-level disclosure check for that fixture.

## Disclosure envelope

The product evidence accepts only route outputs that are consistent with the
project disclosure standard: model-scale outputs, scalar diagnostics, and
approved aggregate summaries may be returned; patient-level identifiers,
matched row indices, row scores, fitted probabilities, BLUPs, cluster vectors,
rank primitives, or other observation-level reconstruction aids must not be
returned to the analyst.

Working quantities used by the algorithms can remain encrypted, secret-shared,
or server-side. The disclosure question in these vignettes is what the product
route returns to the analyst.

The DSLite examples disable trusted-peer pinning because DSLite is an
in-memory validation backend, not an Opal/Rock deployment with persistent peer
identity. That does not relax the method-level disclosure checks.

## Method coverage

| Method | User-facing route | K=2 evidence | K>=3 evidence | Current product path |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| PSI alignment | `ds.vert.align()` | [K=2](validation_psi_k2.html) | [K>=3](validation_psi_kge3.html) | Strict exact alignment counts/status only. |
| Descriptive statistics | `ds.vert.desc()` | [K=2](validation_descriptive_k2.html) | [K>=3](validation_descriptive_kge3.html) | Strict aggregate means and standard deviations. |
| Contingency tests | `ds.vert.chisq()`, `ds.vert.fisher()`, `ds.vert.chisq_cross()` | [K=2](validation_contingency_k2.html) | [K>=3](validation_contingency_kge3.html) | Strict count/test aggregate route. |
| Correlation | `ds.vert.cor()` | [K=2](validation_correlation_k2.html) | [K>=3](validation_correlation_kge3.html) | Practical strict correlation route. |
| PCA | `ds.vert.pca()` | [K=2](validation_pca_k2.html) | [K>=3](validation_pca_kge3.html) | Practical strict eigen/loading route. |
| GLM | `ds.vert.glm()` | [K=2](validation_glm_k2.html) | [K>=3](validation_glm_kge3.html) | Practical strict encrypted-label GLM route. |
| Inference helpers | `ds.vert.confint()`, `ds.vert.wald()`, `ds.vert.contrast()`, `ds.vert.lr()` | [K=2](validation_inference_k2.html) | [K>=3](validation_inference_kge3.html) | Exact algebra on accepted GLM output. |
| LASSO | `ds.vert.lasso_proximal()` | [K=2](validation_lasso_k2.html) | [K>=3](validation_lasso_kge3.html) | Strict proximal route; validation checks the lambda=0 OLS limit. |
| Negative binomial | `ds.vert.nb()` | [K=2](validation_negative_binomial_k2.html) | [K>=3](validation_negative_binomial_kge3.html) | Accurate full-regression theta route. |
| Cox PH | `ds.vert.cox()` | [K=2](validation_cox_k2.html) | [K>=3](validation_cox_kge3.html) | Practical non-disclosive profile/discrete route. |
| LMM | `ds.vert.lmm()` | [K=2](validation_lmm_k2.html) | [K>=3](validation_lmm_kge3.html) | Practical random-intercept route. |
| GEE | `ds.vert.gee()` | [K=2](validation_gee_k2.html) | [K>=3](validation_gee_kge3.html) | Practical aggregate estimating-equation route. |
| GLMM | `ds.vert.glmm()` | [K=2](validation_glmm_k2.html) | [K>=3](validation_glmm_kge3.html) | PQL product route. |
| IPW | `ds.vert.ipw()` | [K=2](validation_ipw_k2.html) | [K>=3](validation_ipw_kge3.html) | High-precision weighted outcome route. |
| MI | `ds.vert.mi()` | [K=2](validation_mi_k2.html) | [K>=3](validation_mi_kge3.html) | Practical pooled coefficient route. |
| Multinomial | `ds.vert.multinom()` | [K=2](validation_multinomial_k2.html) | [K>=3](validation_multinomial_kge3.html) | Joint softmax Newton route. |
| Ordinal | `ds.vert.ordinal()` | [K=2](validation_ordinal_k2.html) | [K>=3](validation_ordinal_kge3.html) | Proportional-odds cumulative probability route. |

## Beaver preprocessing profile evidence

The product supports two Beaver preprocessing profiles. `dealer` is the
efficient profile for governed, semi-honest institutional peers. `iknp` uses
IKNP-style OT-extension so that the two computation parties derive their
triple shares interactively without one party holding the full unsplit triple.
The server-side policy determines which profiles are allowed, preferred or
required; the client negotiates the strictest common profile and fails closed
if no common profile is available.

The following executable articles compare the same route under both profiles.
Each article starts DSLite servers, aligns the same vertically partitioned
fixture, runs the route once with `dealer` and once with `iknp`, compares both
against the same centralized R reference and asserts both the route tolerance
and the profile-to-profile difference.

| Route | Article | Dealer observed | IKNP observed | Profile delta | Tolerance | Runtime ratio | Status |
|---|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---|
| Correlation | [validation_beaver_profile_correlation_k2](validation_beaver_profile_correlation_k2.html) | `1.2441795e-05` | `1.2441795e-05` | `0e+00` | `1e-03` | `9.71` | PASS |
| GLM | [validation_beaver_profile_glm_k2](validation_beaver_profile_glm_k2.html) | `6.2726772e-05` | `6.1290240e-05` | `1.4365328e-06` | `1e-03` | `15.28` | PASS |
| GEE | [validation_beaver_profile_gee_k2](validation_beaver_profile_gee_k2.html) | `1.7860961e-04` | `6.0821320e-05` | `1.1778829e-04` | `1e-03` | `10.88` | PASS |
| Cox PH | [validation_beaver_profile_cox_k2](validation_beaver_profile_cox_k2.html) | `4.8702002e-05` | `4.8702002e-05` | `3.3861802e-15` | `1e-03` | `19.31` | PASS |

## Route-level result snapshot

The following table is copied from the current rendered validation outputs.
It is a static summary; the linked method articles contain the executable code
and the rendered DSLite logs.

| Method | K | Article | Route | Metric | Observed | Tolerance | Tier | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|---:|---:|---|---|
| PSI | K=2 | [validation_psi_k2](validation_psi_k2.html) | `ds.vert.align` | `max(count_delta, correlation_delta)` | 0 | 0 | strict-precise | PASS |
| PSI | K>=3 | [validation_psi_kge3](validation_psi_kge3.html) | `ds.vert.align` | `max(count_delta, correlation_delta)` | 0 | 0 | strict-precise | PASS |
| Descriptive | K=2 | [validation_descriptive_k2](validation_descriptive_k2.html) | `ds.vert.desc` | `max(mean_sd_abs_delta)` | 0 | 0 | strict-precise | PASS |
| Descriptive | K>=3 | [validation_descriptive_kge3](validation_descriptive_kge3.html) | `ds.vert.desc` | `max(mean_sd_abs_delta)` | 0 | 0 | strict-precise | PASS |
| Contingency | K=2 | [validation_contingency_k2](validation_contingency_k2.html) | `ds.vert.chisq / ds.vert.fisher / ds.vert.chisq_cross` | `max(count_or_chisq_delta)` | 0 | 0 | strict-precise | PASS |
| Contingency | K>=3 | [validation_contingency_kge3](validation_contingency_kge3.html) | `ds.vert.chisq / ds.vert.fisher / ds.vert.chisq_cross` | `max(count_or_chisq_delta)` | 0 | 0 | strict-precise | PASS |
| Correlation | K=2 | [validation_correlation_k2](validation_correlation_k2.html) | `ds.vert.cor` | `correlation_max_abs_delta` | 1.24e-05 | 0.001 | strict-practical | PASS |
| Correlation | K>=3 | [validation_correlation_kge3](validation_correlation_kge3.html) | `ds.vert.cor` | `correlation_max_abs_delta` | 1.24e-05 | 0.001 | strict-practical | PASS |
| PCA | K=2 | [validation_pca_k2](validation_pca_k2.html) | `ds.vert.pca` | `max(eigen_loading_abs_delta)` | 3.29e-05 | 0.001 | strict-practical | PASS |
| PCA | K>=3 | [validation_pca_kge3](validation_pca_kge3.html) | `ds.vert.pca` | `max(eigen_loading_abs_delta)` | 3.09e-05 | 0.001 | strict-practical | PASS |
| GLM | K=2 | [validation_glm_k2](validation_glm_k2.html) | `ds.vert.glm` | `coef_max_abs_delta` | 6.24e-05 | 0.001 | strict-practical | PASS |
| GLM | K>=3 | [validation_glm_kge3](validation_glm_kge3.html) | `ds.vert.glm` | `coef_max_abs_delta` | 5.93e-05 | 0.001 | strict-practical | PASS |
| Inference | K=2 | [validation_inference_k2](validation_inference_k2.html) | `ds.vert.confint / ds.vert.wald / ds.vert.contrast / ds.vert.lr` | `algebra_max_abs_delta` | 0 | 0 | strict-precise | PASS |
| Inference | K>=3 | [validation_inference_kge3](validation_inference_kge3.html) | `ds.vert.confint / ds.vert.wald / ds.vert.contrast / ds.vert.lr` | `algebra_max_abs_delta` | 0 | 0 | strict-precise | PASS |
| LASSO | K=2 | [validation_lasso_k2](validation_lasso_k2.html) | `ds.vert.lasso_proximal(lambda=0)` | `lambda0_coef_abs_delta` | 0 | 0 | strict-precise | PASS |
| LASSO | K>=3 | [validation_lasso_kge3](validation_lasso_kge3.html) | `ds.vert.lasso_proximal(lambda=0)` | `lambda0_coef_abs_delta` | 0 | 0 | strict-precise | PASS |
| Negative binomial | K=2 | [validation_negative_binomial_k2](validation_negative_binomial_k2.html) | `ds.vert.nb(method='accurate')` | `coef_max_abs_delta` | 0.0000277 | 0.001 | strict-practical | PASS |
| Negative binomial | K>=3 | [validation_negative_binomial_kge3](validation_negative_binomial_kge3.html) | `ds.vert.nb(method='accurate')` | `coef_max_abs_delta` | 0.0000317 | 0.001 | strict-practical | PASS |
| Cox | K=2 | [validation_cox_k2](validation_cox_k2.html) | `ds.vert.cox(method='profile')` | `coef_max_abs_delta` | 4.87e-05 | 0.001 | strict-practical | PASS |
| Cox | K>=3 | [validation_cox_kge3](validation_cox_kge3.html) | `ds.vert.cox(method='profile')` | `coef_max_abs_delta` | 4.87e-05 | 0.001 | strict-practical | PASS |
| LMM | K=2 | [validation_lmm_k2](validation_lmm_k2.html) | `ds.vert.lmm` | `fixed_effect_max_abs_delta` | 0.0006448 | 0.001 | strict-practical | PASS |
| LMM | K>=3 | [validation_lmm_kge3](validation_lmm_kge3.html) | `ds.vert.lmm` | `fixed_effect_max_abs_delta` | 0.0001614 | 0.001 | strict-practical | PASS |
| GEE | K=2 | [validation_gee_k2](validation_gee_k2.html) | `ds.vert.gee(corstr='independence')` | `coef_max_abs_delta` | 6.07e-05 | 0.001 | strict-practical | PASS |
| GEE | K>=3 | [validation_gee_kge3](validation_gee_kge3.html) | `ds.vert.gee(corstr='independence')` | `coef_max_abs_delta` | 6e-05 | 0.001 | strict-practical | PASS |
| GLMM | K=2 | [validation_glmm_k2](validation_glmm_k2.html) | `ds.vert.glmm(method='pql')` | `fixed_effect_max_abs_delta_and_pql_quality` | 0.0005547 | 0.001 | strict-practical | PASS |
| GLMM | K>=3 | [validation_glmm_kge3](validation_glmm_kge3.html) | `ds.vert.glmm(method='pql')` | `fixed_effect_max_abs_delta_and_pql_quality` | 0.0005547 | 0.001 | strict-practical | PASS |
| IPW | K=2 | [validation_ipw_k2](validation_ipw_k2.html) | `ds.vert.ipw(precision='high')` | `weighted_outcome_coef_abs_delta` | 0.0001785 | 0.001 | strict-practical | PASS |
| IPW | K>=3 | [validation_ipw_kge3](validation_ipw_kge3.html) | `ds.vert.ipw(precision='high')` | `weighted_outcome_coef_abs_delta` | 0.0001785 | 0.001 | strict-practical | PASS |
| MI | K=2 | [validation_mi_k2](validation_mi_k2.html) | `ds.vert.mi` | `pooled_coef_abs_delta` | 1.69e-05 | 0.001 | strict-practical | PASS |
| MI | K>=3 | [validation_mi_kge3](validation_mi_kge3.html) | `ds.vert.mi` | `pooled_coef_abs_delta` | 1.66e-05 | 0.001 | strict-practical | PASS |
| Multinomial | K=2 | [validation_multinomial_k2](validation_multinomial_k2.html) | `ds.vert.multinom` | `class_probability_max_abs_delta` | 3.23e-05 | 0.001 | strict-practical | PASS |
| Multinomial | K>=3 | [validation_multinomial_kge3](validation_multinomial_kge3.html) | `ds.vert.multinom` | `class_probability_max_abs_delta` | 3.28e-05 | 0.001 | strict-practical | PASS |
| Ordinal | K=2 | [validation_ordinal_k2](validation_ordinal_k2.html) | `ds.vert.ordinal` | `cumulative_probability_max_abs_delta` | 1.61e-05 | 0.001 | strict-practical | PASS |
| Ordinal | K>=3 | [validation_ordinal_kge3](validation_ordinal_kge3.html) | `ds.vert.ordinal` | `cumulative_probability_max_abs_delta` | 3.98e-05 | 0.001 | strict-practical | PASS |
